mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns exceed 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS rises, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that override earnings growth, causing sustained negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of cases when UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and consumer prices indicate robust demand outpacing supply constraints, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of triggered months, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production acceleration, short-term momentum in equity markets amplifies positive factory output data, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or Rank IC drops below 0.015 when filtered specifically for post-shock regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal expanding construction activity and consumer confidence, which directly boosts capital goods demand and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST increases coincide with negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of observations, or the correlation coefficient remains below 0.05 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.4%, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of high-inflation months, or the threshold effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 0.4% during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models with F-test p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.5% signals an overheating economy that precedes a growth slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: High GDP growth periods produce positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of instances during late-cycle phases, or the negative relationship reverses sign in out-of-sample testing.
